1.11b: Refer to contact details in section 1.11a.
1.7: Lending is prohibited if the borrower and mortgagor are different entities.
1.14: Firm may act if a separate fee earner of equal standing or a partner acts for the lender.
1.15: Firm may act if a separate fee earner of equal standing or a partner acts for the lender.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's solicitor's name and address is required.
3.1.4: The seller must have legal representation.
3.1.5: Certified copies of identity documentation for borrowers and signatories must be retained for lender inspection.
3.2.3: Notification of the seller's solicitor's name and address is required.
3.2.4: The seller must have legal representation.
4.1: Valuation reports are provided with the mortgage offer unless no physical valuation was obtained.
Required checks must be performed against mortgage offer information if no valuation report is provided.
4.3: Contact Legals@landbay.co.uk regarding incorrect valuation assumptions.
4.5b: Certificates of Title must be submitted online via the 'Satisfy conditions and submit eCoT' link in the instruction email.
5.1.1: Contact Legals@landbay.co.uk if the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months.
5.2.1: Contact Legals@landbay.co.uk if the seller is not the registered proprietor.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are not required but must be retained for information.
Lending is prohibited for properties in high flood risk areas.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted at the conveyancer's risk.
Minimum £2 million Professional Indemnity Insurance required.
Gross loan must be below the indemnity limit.
Provider must be PCCB regulated and CoPSO registered.
Policy must cover both lender and borrower.
5.4.6: Search insurance is restricted to remortgages of single self-contained properties.
Excludes HMOs, Multi-Unit buildings, and unencumbered properties.
Excludes properties constructed after a registered charge on the C Register.
Search validation indemnity is not accepted for searches older than 6 months.
5.5.3a: Planning indemnity accepted for alterations over 7 years old.
Building regulations indemnity accepted for alterations over 10 years old.
HMO and MUB properties must have all required consents and licenses.
Block flats require a copy of a current Fire Risk Assessment (FRA).
Private drainage (septic tanks) requires legal rights of access, maintenance covenants, and insurance.
5.5.3b: Copies of planning permission are not required by the lender but must be retained on file.
5.5.3c: Copies of building regulation consents are not required by the lender but must be retained on file.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development must be obtained and retained on file.
5.5.4: Unilateral Notices for Manorial Rights require indemnity insurance covering 110% of the property value or purchase price (whichever is higher).
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds accepted up to a maximum of 15% of the property area.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are not accepted as security.
5.8.1: Lending allowed if borrower has a lease of a flat and owns the freehold, provided other flats are subject to long leases.
Borrower occupation is prohibited as the loan is for Buy-to-Let.
5.8.5: South Tyneside Leases are not accepted.
North Tyneside Leases require a legal charge over both leasehold and freehold titles.
5.10.1: Restrictions on using the property as a single unit are prohibited.
Lessees must not be in breach of covenants regarding letting.
5.13.1: Deed of gift indemnity policy required if the borrower is not providing the full balance.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term: 65 years at the end of the mortgage term.
5.14.9: New build ground rent is capped at 0.1% of property value.
Existing ground rent is capped at £250 per annum (outside London) or £1,000 per annum (inside London).
Escalation via doubling at 5, 10, or 15-year intervals is prohibited.
Indemnity insurance for ground rent is not accepted; a Deed of Variation is required.
Maisonettes with unsatisfactory repair obligations require indemnity insurance for 110% of the value or price.
5.14.10: Service charge cap: 1.0% of property value for loans up to £499,999.99.
Service charge cap: 1.5% of property value for loans of £500,000.00 and over.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance is accepted for lease defects where a Deed of Variation is not possible.
5.14.12: Clear ground rent and service charge receipts are required.
5.14.13: Receipted copy of notice or evidence of service is required.
5.14.15a: Contact Legals@landbay.co.uk regarding absentee or insolvent landlords.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absentee landlords is accepted if it allows for an unqualified title certificate.
5.14.17a: Title documents should be retained by the firm and not sent to the lender unless requested post-completion.
5.14.17b: Buildings higher than 11m require an EWS1 rating of A1 only.
5.15.2a: Contact Legals@landbay.co.uk regarding management company issues.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates should be retained on file.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms should be retained on file.
5.15.2d: Memorandum and articles of association should be retained on file.
5.16.2: Contact Legals@landbay.co.uk if unable to certify search entries.
5.17.5a: Power of Attorney must not be used to execute lender documents.
POAs used for other documents must be sent to the lender after completion.
5.17.5b: Statutory declaration of non-revocation of Power of Attorney must be sent after completion.
5.19.1: Affordable housing, shared equity, and shared ownership schemes are not accepted.
5.20.1: Properties with solar panel (PV) leasing are not accepted.

6.4.4: Completed UK Finance Disclosure of Incentives form is required.
6.4.5: Contact Legals@landbay.co.uk if control of purchase money is restricted.
6.5.1: Vacant possession is required if the vendor is a family member.
Non-related tenancies require assignment of the AST to the borrower.
6.6.1: Tenancies must comply with Clause 7.8 of the Loan Agreement.
Lender must be notified immediately of non-compliant tenancies.
6.6.3: Certified copies of tenancy agreements must be available upon request; originals must be retained in the conveyancing file.
6.6.4: HMOs are accepted subject to compliance with national regulations and local planning/licensing.
6.7.1: Accepted warranty schemes: ARK, Advantage HCI, BLP, Build-Zone, Checkmate UK, Global Home Warranties, Home Proof, ICW, LABC, NHBC, Premier Guarantee (no reservations), Protek, Q Policy, Zurich Municipal.
6.7.2: All relevant warranty documentation must be submitted.
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes must be kept with title deeds post-completion.
6.7.4: Lending allowed without warranty if monitored by a professional consultant with current Professional Indemnity insurance.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificates must be sent to the lender after completion.
6.8.1: Unadopted roads or sewers require enforceable maintenance rights, absence of disputes, and appropriate rights of way.
6.9.1: Full pedestrian and vehicular access rights required.
Maintenance liabilities exceeding £250 per annum (excluding service charges) must be reported.
6.10.2: Retentions are not permitted at completion.
6.11.1: Contact Legals@landbay.co.uk regarding redevelopment or road proposals.
6.12.1: Overage agreements and resale restrictions are not accepted.
6.13.1: Properties with undischarged improvement or repair grants are not accepted unless the council confirms in writing that repayment is not required.
7.3: Residential status is limited to tenants (Buy-to-Let only).
7.4: Contact Legals@landbay.co.uk regarding accuracy of disclosed information.
8.1: Conveyancers are prohibited from advising guarantors; independent legal advice is required.
9.1: Indemnity policies do not need to be sent but must ensure a good and marketable title.
9.2: Indemnity limit: 110% of the higher of the property valuation or purchase price.
10.2a: Mortgage advance paid via electronic transfer.
10.2b: Standard notice for Certificate of Title: 5 working days.
Notice for brought-forward completion: 3 working days.
10.7: Contact Legals@landbay.co.uk immediately for delayed completions.
10.10: Funds may be held for up to 3 working days only with prior approval from Legals@landbay.co.uk.
10.11: Interest is charged from the date of advance at the rate specified in the Mortgage Offer.
